title: How Facebook's Algorithmic Tweaks Affected Engagement with News URLs over Time
snippet: Detailed blog coverage summarizing Professor Fabio Giglietto's paper presentation at the International Communication Association (ICA 2026) conference in Cape Town. The write-up outlines Giglietto's analysis of a massive US Facebook URL dataset, explaining how algorithm shifts introduce partisan penalties and affect news quality distribution.
description: A detailed third-party blog post on snurb.info summarizing Professor Fabio Giglietto's research presentation at the ICA 2026 conference regarding Facebook's algorithmic impact on news URL engagement.

title: SMSociety 2026 Conference Programme
snippet: An event announcement naming Fabio Giglietto as a featured speaker and roundtable panelist for the session 'Meta Content Library Community Roundtable: Where to for Social Media Research?' at the International Conference on Social Media & Society in Glasgow.
description: Fabio Giglietto is featured as a roundtable panelist discussing the Meta Content Library and the future of social media research at the International Conference on Social Media & Society.

title: Indagine urbinate: "Così l’algoritmo influenza il voto" - Il Resto del Carlino

title: Algoritmi e democrazia, il caso Meta secondo lo studio di UniUrb
snippet: This press article details the public impact of Fabio Giglietto's study 'A Pretty Blunt Approach,' which analyzed Meta's content algorithms. The article explains how Giglietto's findings became the center of a European Parliament inquiry and were featured on the Rai3 investigative television program 'Report.'
description: An article discussing the political and public fallout of Professor Giglietto's research on Meta's algorithms, including its feature on Rai3's 'Report' and subsequent European Parliament inquiries.

title: Facebook, troppa visibilità a estremisti e complottisti: ecco come impatta Meta sulle elezioni
snippet: Published on January 9, 2026, this journalistic piece covers Giglietto's study analyzing over 2.5 million Facebook posts from 2021 to 2025. It highlights how Meta's algorithmic feed suppression policies severely reduced the reach of elected Italian parliamentarians while inadvertently boosting extremist and anti-EU accounts.
description: An article covering Professor Fabio Giglietto's research analyzing over 2.5 million Facebook posts, focusing on how political extremist pages bypassed Meta's filters to gain visibility during elections.

title: Facebook premia gli estremisti: la ricerca dell'Università di Urbino - Lumsanews
Meno contenuti politici nei feed, la stretta di Meta è stata meno efficace contro gli estremisti - Il Sole 24 ORE

title: Secondo uno studio dell'università di Urbino l'algoritmo di Facebook ora punisce la politica ufficiale e premia gli estremisti antieuropei. Pd e FdI verso una legge bipartisan sulle piattaforme
snippet: A prominent news article from January 7, 2026, highlighting Fabio Giglietto's newly published study on the asymmetric impact of Meta's political content policies. It covers how the academic paper spurred discussions in the Italian Parliament toward establishing potential bipartisan regulations on digital platform transparency.
description: An article from Open.online covering a study by Fabio Giglietto of the University of Urbino regarding the asymmetric impact of Facebook's algorithm on political content, which has prompted discussion of bipartisan platform regulation in Italy.
